joke: Why don’t skeletons fight each other? They don’t have the guts.
Why did the tomato blush? Because it saw the salad dressing.
Why did the bicycle fall over? Because it was two-tired.
Why did the math book look sad? Because it had too many problems.
Why did the astronaut need some alone time? They needed space.
Why did the computer catch a cold? It left its Windows open.
What does a cloud wear under its raincoat? Thunderwear.
Why did the scarecrow win an award? Because he was outstanding in his field.
Why did the musician bring a ladder? To reach the high notes.
Why are elevators so good at jokes? They work on many levels.
Why did the golfer bring two pairs of pants? In case they got a hole in one.
Why did the broom get promoted? It swept the competition.
Why was the photographer always calm? They knew how to focus.
Why did the calendar get nervous? Its days were numbered.
Why did the duck become a detective? It always quacked the case.
Why did the mirror get invited to every party? It always reflected well on the host.
Why did the lawyer bring a pencil to court? To draw a conclusion.
Why did the chair get promoted? It always supported the team.
Why was the envelope so confident? It knew how to address any situation.
Why did the suitcase apply for therapy? It had too much emotional baggage.
I told my boss three companies were after me: the gas, electric, and water companies.
I started a hide-and-seek club, but good members are hard to find.
I have a joke about construction, but I’m still working on it.
My friend says I’m indecisive. I’m not sure how I feel about that.
Why did the dictionary need a nap? It had been defining things all day.
I entered a napping contest, but I slept through the finals.
Why did the candle quit its job? It was burned out.
Why did the origami class close? It folded.
I joined a procrastination club. We’re planning to hold our first meeting someday.
Why did the balloon avoid arguments? It didn’t want things to blow up.
